action.hypothesis: JumpReLU at K=128 may still benefit from a reduced but nonzero auxiliary dead-feature objective, and lowering AUXK_ALPHA from 0.03125 to 0.01 could preserve feature health while reducing the reconstruction penalty seen at the baseline auxk weight.
action.summary: Keep the best-quality JumpReLU K=128 configuration fixed at LR=1.6e-3 and continue the auxk sweep with AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 after the zero-auxk ablation underperformed.
action.self_review: This follows directly from the failed AUXK_ALPHA=0 test and probes the obvious intermediate point without changing any other variable. The main risk is that auxk may simply not be the limiting factor, but 0.01 is the most informative remaining near-baseline setting before moving on to expansion-factor sweeps.
notes_to_memory: AUXK_ALPHA=0 was clearly worse than the K=128 JumpReLU anchor, so some auxiliary pressure appears necessary for good proxy quality.
Round 15 tests a reduced-but-nonzero auxk setting at 0.01 on the strongest JumpReLU K=128 baseline.
This round is intended to separate 'auxk is useful' from 'the current auxk weight is optimal' before moving on to expansion-factor sweeps.
next_hypotheses: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then refine auxk locally around that smaller nonzero setting.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 is still worse, finish the planned auxk sweep with 0.0625 only if there is evidence higher auxk may help; otherwise move to EXPANSION_FACTOR=16.
If auxk continues to underperform, keep K=128 and LR=1.6e-3 fixed and begin expansion-factor exploration to test whether capacity, not regularization, is now the main bottleneck.
